codeReview.summary: The change adds assertion-backed checks, but key VAL-DASH-008 requirements are still not covered by observable failure-path assertions: switch-failure transitions are not actually induced/asserted, launch assertions are satisfied by a DEBUG stub rather than production behavior, and load-error verification remains indirect/generic.

issues.description: No view-level test forces a switch failure transition and asserts the required failure announcement. The new switch tests only exercise success/same-profile paths via `view.testTriggerSwitch()`, so the feature requirement to verify success/failure/recovery announcement transitions is not met for switch failure.

`testTriggerLaunch(profile:)` emits a hardcoded launch error string instead of exercising the real launch path, and `test_viewLevel_launchSuccessAnnounces` accepts either success or error text (`launched successfully` OR `Launch error`) at line 1158 in the test file. This can pass without validating real success/failure accessibility behavior.

`test_viewLevel_loadErrorAnnounces` explicitly does not create an error condition and only asserts a generic result (`loaded` OR `Error`, line 1212). This remains indirect and does not provide assertion-backed verification of the actual error announcement transition.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `switcher-ui-worker` skill procedure requires broad validation (`scripts/test-openburnbar-app.sh`) and manual UI sanity checks, but this handoff reports only scoped `xcodebuild test` + `build-for-testing` while `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/switcher-ui-worker/SKILL.md (Work Procedure steps 6-7) vs mission handoff 2026-04-09T22-47-52-536Z__fast-surfaces-fix-dashboard-switch-failure-a11y-announcement__6778f0fe-6f4a-45ed-af01-98f8e2171db3.json verification.commandsRun and skillFeedback.

summary: Reviewed commit 823c4d42363244eceef57553eaea078b5b0fc2d2 and marked this feature as fail: tests improved, but they still do not directly validate required failure-path accessibility announcements for switch/load transitions and include a launch assertion path that can pass without exercising production behavior.
